curricula.name: Cambridge
curricula.blurb: Cambridge Primary, Lower Secondary, IGCSE & O Level.

bands.label: Ages 10–12
bands.blurb: Foundations, with lots of examples and encouragement.

topics.title: Parts of Speech: Naming and Doing Words
objectives: Tell the difference between nouns, verbs and adjectives
Spot these word types in a sentence
Use each kind of word to make your own sentences clearer
body: Every sentence is built from different kinds of words, and three of the most important ones are nouns, verbs and adjectives. Once you can spot them, reading and writing both get easier, because you understand the job each word is doing.

A noun is a naming word. It names a person, a place, an animal or a thing. 'Maya', 'school', 'tiger' and 'pencil' are all nouns. A quick trick: if you can put 'the' or 'a' in front of a word and it still makes sense, it is probably a noun, like 'the school' or 'a pencil'.

A verb is a doing or being word. It tells you the action in a sentence, like 'run', 'jump', 'read' or 'is'. Try this test: can someone actually do it? You can 'jump' and you can 'read', so those are verbs. In 'The tiger runs fast', the verb is 'runs'.

An adjective is a describing word. It tells you more about a noun, like 'happy', 'tall', 'red' or 'noisy'. In 'the noisy classroom', the adjective 'noisy' describes the noun 'classroom'. Good writers use adjectives to paint a clear picture in the reader's mind, but they choose them carefully so the writing does not feel crowded.
keyPoints: A noun names a person, place, animal or thing
A verb shows an action or a state of being
Most sentences use all three together
Try the simple tests: 'the/a' for nouns, 'can you do it?' for verbs
topics.example: In the sentence 'The brave firefighter climbed the tall ladder', the nouns are 'firefighter' and 'ladder', the verb is 'climbed', and the adjectives are 'brave' and 'tall'.
topics.summary: Nouns name, verbs do, and adjectives describe. Spotting them helps you read closely and write clearly.

quiz.text: Which word is a noun in: 'The puppy barked loudly'?
quiz.explanation: 'Puppy' names an animal, so it is a noun.

quiz.text: Which word is a verb in: 'She paints beautiful pictures'?
quiz.explanation: 'Paints' is the action, so it is the verb.

quiz.text: Which word is an adjective in: 'A cold wind blew'?
quiz.explanation: 'Cold' describes the noun 'wind', so it is an adjective.

quiz.text: An adjective is a word that…
How many verbs are in: 'He ran and jumped'?
quiz.explanation: 'Ran' and 'jumped' are both actions — two verbs.
